tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine and potassium, low urine specific gravity, significant proteinuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—may need to confirm accuracy or consider anemia/hemoglobinopathy. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Lipid profile is within acceptable limits. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and possible underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
